steps.title: Identify what the claim needs
steps.content: The student's claim directly compares viewing times: people spend more time viewing art in museums than online. To support this comparison, evidence must provide actual time measurements from both locations.

steps.title: Find the choice with viewing-time evidence
steps.content:
Choice A — Most people spend about three minutes viewing *The Card Game* at the museum and only about one minute viewing it online. provides specific viewing times from both settings: three minutes at the museum versus one minute online.

This directly measures the comparison the claim asserts. The alternatives address the work's online availability, visitor origins, and museum collections—none of which measure actual viewing time.

That matches Choice A — Most people spend about three minutes viewing *The Card Game* at the museum and only about one minute viewing it online..
